tool_design.role_in_intervention: Full operating standard behind the quick-response card, including drills and post-incident review.
tool_design.usage_moment: At rollout, drills, and manager refresher reviews
tool_design.expected_output: Issued excursion SOP with drill scorecards and clarified response roles

failure_warnings.warning: SOP is issued but not practiced through drills.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Staff know of the standard but still panic under pressure.
early_signals: Card is visible but confidence is low

failure_warnings.warning: SOP no longer matches real contingency options.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The team follows an outdated response path.
prevention: Review after equipment or staffing changes

content:
# Temperature Excursion SOP & Drill Guide

**Tool ID:** T003-05  
**Block:** B003 (Temperature Excursion Response)  
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)  
**Level:** L2  
**Format:** SOP  
**Role:** Manager / Owner  
**Time Required:** 20 minutes to issue, 10 minutes per drill  
**Version:** 2.0

---
## What This Tool Is For

Use this SOP to define the full venue response when a unit fails, stock is at risk, or an excursion happens outside a simple one-line fix.

This is the deeper operating guide behind the response card.

---
## Incident Sequence

| Step | Standard |
|---|---|
| 1 | Trigger identified and confirmed |
| 2 | Unit protected from unnecessary opening |
| 3 | Manager notified |
| 4 | Stock assessed using [T003-03] |
| 5 | Incident recorded on [T003-02] |
| 6 | Engineer / owner / back-up storage activated if needed |
| 7 | Root cause and prevention captured in [T003-04] |

---
## Roles During An Excursion

| Role | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| First finder | Trigger the response and notify manager |
| Manager / duty lead | Lead the assessment and stock decision |
| Owner | Support major loss, supplier, or equipment decisions |
| Engineer / contractor | Repair or advise on recovery |

---
## Stock Transfer Rule

If safe stock must be moved:
- use a known compliant back-up fridge or freezer
- minimise door-open time during transfer
- mark transferred stock clearly
- avoid mixing questionable stock with known-safe stock without a decision record

---
## Drill Guide

Run a short drill at least quarterly.

### Suggested scenarios
- Main milk fridge found at `11C` at opening
- Freezer door left ajar overnight
- Display fridge warm during peak service
- Power cut affects more than one unit

### Drill scorecard

| Check | Yes / No | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Team identified the trigger quickly | | |
| Manager was notified fast | | |
| Stock decision used the matrix, not guesswork | | |
| Incident record would have been completed | | |
| Team knew where back-up storage was | | |

---
## Post-Incident Review

After every real excursion ask:
- what happened
- why it happened
- what we controlled badly
- what we need to change before the next one

Keep the review short, factual, and action-focused.

---
## Connected Resources

**Core tools in this block:**
- T003-01 - Temperature Excursion Response Card
- T003-02 - Excursion Incident Report Form
- T003-03 - Time-Temperature Stock Assessment Matrix
- T003-04 - Excursion Trend Tracker & Root Cause Log

**Related blocks:**
- B001 - Daily Temperature Monitoring
- B002 - Cold Chain Management SOP
- B004 - Cold Chain Excellence Dashboard

---

*Tool Version 2.0 - the full incident standard behind the quick card.*
